evals.name: direct-status-query
evals.prompt: Show me the current product status for this project. The feature catalog lives at .garura/product/scope/features.yaml. Give me the domain -> capability -> feature tree with per-feature status.

assertions.text: Output contains feature AM-F001 on its own row
Every feature row ends with a bracketed status like [released], [rollout], [planned], [development], or [cleanup]
No feature row contains '[#' (no feature has an issue field, so the issue slot must be entirely omitted)
Empty capabilities l4-signal-driven and l5-goal-driven render '(no features yet)'
Indentation uses exactly 2 spaces per level (domain col 0, capability col 2, feature col 4)
Output contains no summary line (no 'features_total', no 'Summary:', no total counts rolled up)
Domain and capability rows contain only the slug — no status in brackets on those rows

evals.name: natural-language-where-are-we
evals.prompt: where are we on the product right now? i want to see what's built vs planned. features live at .garura/product/scope/features.yaml

assertions.text: Casual phrasing still triggers the structured tree format (not prose summary)
Output contains the domain slug 'agentic-methodology' as a top-level row
Output contains the domain slug 'engineering-experience' as a top-level row
Status strings appear verbatim in brackets (e.g., [rollout] not [partial], [released] not [live])
No prose narration or bullet-list summary wraps the tree

evals.name: specific-domain-followup
evals.prompt: Give me the product state tree from .garura/product/scope/features.yaml. Do NOT summarize or narrate -- just the tree. Preserve YAML order, don't sort. If a feature has no `issue` field, skip the `: [#123]` slot entirely (no empty brackets).

assertions.text: No feature row contains '[#' (issue slot entirely omitted)
No feature row contains '[]' or empty brackets for the issue slot
Every feature row ends with a status bracket
Features appear in document order from features.yaml (AM-F001 before AM-F002, EO-F001 before EO-F005)
Output is pure tree — no headers, no prose, no totals, no 'Summary:' line
Uses exact 2-space indent per level — not tabs, not 4 spaces, not tree-drawing characters like ├── or └──
